name: CurveBench-Easy
description: CurveBench-Easy is the foundational split of the CurveBench benchmark. It contains 300 hand-drawn images of disjoint curves, each paired with a rooted-tree annotation encoding the nested containment relationships between regions. The Easy split covers all rooted-tree topologies with up to 6 nodes, with at least 2 visually distinct representations per topology. It is divided into three difficulty levels based on the number of curves in each image.
rai:dataCollection: Images were manually hand-drawn by the research team. All rooted-tree topologies with up to 6 nodes were enumerated and at least 2 visually distinct renderings per topology were produced. Difficulty levels correspond to increasing numbers of curves per image.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Ground-truth rooted trees were generated using an automated OpenCV contour-based extraction pipeline that traces boundary curves and assembles parent-child containment relationships.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Every annotation was individually human-verified to confirm structural correctness, catch missed or merged regions, and remove images with ambiguous contours.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset does not contain personal data, faces, biometric information, private text, or human-subject records. All images are synthetic hand-drawn diagrams.
rai:dataBiases: Hand-authored examples reflect the drawing styles and visual conventions of the annotators. The topological coverage is exhaustive for up to 6 nodes, but visual diversity per topology is limited.
rai:dataLimitations: The dataset evaluates rooted region-containment topology only. It does not measure metric geometry, 3D structure, temporal dynamics, or real-world semantic understanding.
rai:dataUseCases: Research evaluation of visual topological reasoning and verifiable RLVR training for vision-language models.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Methods trained on this dataset could contribute to automated map vectorization or geospatial analysis systems. Researchers should be aware of potential surveillance or military downstream applications.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Images were manually hand-drawn by the research team between 2024-09 and 2025-01 using digital drawing software (no real-world or third-party imagery). Coverage was driven by combinatorial enumeration of all rooted-tree topologies with up to 6 nodes, with at least 2 visually distinct renderings per topology. Preprocessing: Each PNG/JPEG was kept at native author resolution; no augmentation, filtering, or resizing was applied beyond white-background normalisation. Data annotation: Ground-truth rooted trees were produced by an automated OpenCV contour-based extraction pipeline that traces boundary curves and assembles parent-child containment relationships, encoded as edge lists [[parent, child], ...] with node 0 fixed as the outermost (background) region. Quality control: Every annotation was independently human-verified by the same research team to confirm structural correctness; ambiguous or noisy contours were either corrected or removed (no formal multi-annotator agreement score was computed because annotation was algorithmic and verified, not crowd-sourced). Tools and platforms used: digital drawing software for image creation, OpenCV (Python) for contour extraction, NetworkX for tree validation, and Hugging Face Datasets for hosting and distribution.

distribution.name: repo
distribution.description: The CurveBench-Easy Hugging Face git repository. The sha256 field is the canonical Croissant placeholder for git-backed repositories per https://github.com/mlcommons/croissant/issues/80, since a git tree does not have a single content hash.

distribution.name: parquet-files-for-config-default
distribution.description: Auto-converted Parquet files for the default config of CurveBench-Easy on the refs/convert/parquet ref. One file per split.

recordSet.name: easy-examples
recordSet.description: All 300 CurveBench-Easy examples with image, node count, and rooted-tree edge list.

field.name: image
field.description: Hand-drawn PNG or JPEG image showing disjoint closed curves representing nested regions. The outermost (background) region is node 0.

field.name: num_nodes
field.description: Total number of nodes in the rooted tree, including the implicit root (node 0 = outermost/background region). Equals the number of closed regions plus one.

field.name: tree
field.description: Edge list of the rooted tree as an array of [parent, child] integer pairs. 0-indexed; node 0 is always the outermost (background) region. Example: [[0,1],[1,2]] means region 1 is inside region 0, and region 2 is inside region 1.

recordSet.name: splits
recordSet.description: Split definitions for CurveBench-Easy across 3 difficulty levels (level_1, level_2, level_3) and train/validation/test subsets.

field.name: level
field.description: Difficulty level (1=fewest curves, 3=most complex). 0 for 'total_*' splits.

field.name: size
field.description: Number of examples in this split.
